steps.text: A margin of error defines a symmetric range around the sample statistic. With sample mean 37 and margin of error 3, the confidence interval is $$37 \pm 3$$
Compute the bounds: lower = $37 - 3 = 34$, upper = $37 + 3 = 40$. The interval is $[34, 40]$.
The most appropriate conclusion is that the population mean lies within this interval, not definitively above or below 37.

• A — “Less than 37” ignores values from 37 to 40 that are in the interval. Eliminated.
  • B — “Greater than 37” ignores values from 34 to 37 that are in the interval. Eliminated.
  • C — “Between 34 and 40” matches exactly $37 \pm 3$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • D — “Less than 34 or greater than 40” describes values outside the interval. Eliminated.
